attachment.content:
- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- sam-template: Use whenever an AWS SAM template.yaml is being created or modified in the dns-de-automation lambda style, or any SAM template that integrates with serverless-access-manager. Trigger on direct requests ("add a new lambda", "update template.yaml", "add an SQS target to the access manager"), AND proactively whenever Claude is implementing code changes that end up adding or modifying a SAM template.yaml as a side-effect (for example: adding a new Lambda env var, wiring a new event source, adding an IAM permission, introducing a new SAM resource). If the implementation touches template.yaml at all, invoke this skill before writing the change.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
title:	DATA-4715: Archive audit logs to S3 on successful erasure
state:	open
author:	STyagi7
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	LyndonHB
comments:	6
number:	62
url:	https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/-/merge_requests/62
--
## What

Implements audit log archival to S3 when an erasure request is successfully processed. Once a request reaches the `done` state, the process Lambda materialises all DynamoDB audit log entries for that id to S3 as a gzipped JSON Lines object (`{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz`), then deletes the rows from DynamoDB. The existing `FallbackAuditLogReader` (used by the reporter Lambda) seamlessly serves reads from S3 after archival with no changes needed on the read path.

Also fixes two gaps surfaced during code review: a missing 409 response for duplicate erasure request IDs, and `BatchWriteItem` throttle failures silently dropping deletes.

Key files: `audit_logging.py`, `request_processor.py`, `processor_lambda.py`, `creator_lambda.py`, `template.yaml`, `README.md`.

## Why

DATA-4715 — audit log entries were accumulating in DynamoDB indefinitely with no archival path. The S3 fallback reader already existed but had no writer. This ticket closes that gap and reduces long-term DynamoDB table growth.

## Changes

**Audit log archival (core feature)**
- Added `S3AuditLogWriter` — symmetric counterpart to the existing `S3AuditLogReader`, writes gzipped JSONL to the same key format (`{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz`)
- Added `DynamoDBAuditLogger.delete_logs()` — batch-deletes all entries for an id using `BatchWriteItem` in chunks of 25, with capped exponential backoff retry on `UnprocessedItems` (up to 5 attempts) to prevent silent partial deletes under throttling
- Added `AuditArchiver` — orchestrates S3-write-then-DynamoDB-delete with safe ordering: if S3 write fails, DynamoDB rows are preserved and reads remain correct
- Added `get_audit_log_archiver()` factory wired from env vars
- `RequestProcessor` accepts an optional `AuditArchiver`; triggers archival after `process_request` completes when `request.state == "done"` and not in dry-run mode; archival failures are caught and logged without re-raising (request is already `done`)
- `processor_lambda.py` constructs the archiver at startup, gracefully degrades to `None` if env vars are absent
- `template.yaml`: added `ProcessS3WritePolicy` (`s3:PutObject` on the audit bucket/prefix) and `AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET`/`AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX` env vars to `ProcessFunction`, mirroring the existing `ReporterS3ReadPolicy` pattern

**Bug fixes**
- `creator_lambda.py`: `ConditionalCheckFailedException` from DynamoDB now returns HTTP 409 Conflict instead of 500, so callers can distinguish duplicate IDs from infrastructure errors
- `request_processor.py`: fixed double-assignment bug in `_transition_request` (`message = message = " due to: " + reason` → `message = message + " due to: " + reason`) that was corrupting audit messages when a `reason` was supplied
- `request_processor.py`: added `case "done": pass` so the match is not a dead-end when a phase transitions to `done` within the same `process_request` call

**Tests (new file + extensions)**
- New `tests/test_s3_audit_log_writer.py` — moto-backed round-trip tests confirming writer and reader are format-compatible
- Extended `tests/test_audit_logging.py` — unit tests for `S3AuditLogWriter`, `AuditArchiver` ordering (S3 before Dynamo; no delete if S3 raises; no-op on empty), `delete_logs` chunking and `UnprocessedItems` retry
- Extended `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` — moto integration tests for `delete_logs` and a test confirming DynamoDB entries survive when S3 is unavailable
- Extended `tests/test_request_processor.py` — archiver triggered on `done`, not triggered in dry-run, exception does not propagate
- Extended `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` — 409 on duplicate id, 500 on other `ClientError` codes

**Housekeeping**
- `README.md`: documented `POST /erasure-request` (including 409), audit log lifecycle, and all five process Lambda env vars
- `TODO.md`: ticked off audit log S3 materialisation; added `need-more-detail` handling as an open item

--
comments/notes:

LyndonHB commented 2026-05-26 15:07:21.918 +0000 UTC
I think this retry stuff would be best isolated a little more into its own code.

I am also unsure at a glance what the time.sleep call does. Why are we doing a left bitwise shift?

LyndonHB commented 2026-05-26 15:07:22.147 +0000 UTC
What's the point in this empty case?

LyndonHB commented 2026-05-26 15:07:22.244 +0000 UTC
Should this not be called by the `case "done"` branch?

LyndonHB commented 2026-05-27 13:34:46.468 +0000 UTC
Perhaps sort by timestamp so this is consistent.

LyndonHB commented 2026-05-27 13:34:46.753 +0000 UTC
@LyndonHB We should take a note and make request_repo throw a more sensible exception so it is easier to know what has happened (i.e. a duplicate key was raised)

LyndonHB commented 2026-05-27 13:34:46.976 +0000 UTC
Might want to move this around in the README.

A new version of glab has been released: v1.90.0 -> v1.100.0
https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/cli/-/releases/v1.100.0

## What

Once a request reaches the `done` state, the process Lambda materialises all DynamoDB audit log entries for that id to S3 as a gzipped JSON Lines object (`{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz`), then deletes the rows from DynamoDB. ## Changes

**Audit log archival (core feature)**

- Added `get_audit_log_archiver()` factory wired from env vars

content.thinking: Let me get more details on these comments, especially to understand which ones are resolved vs unresolved, and what code they're referring to.

content.text: Let me get the full inline comment details with more context.

input.command:
glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/62/discussions 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
for d in data:
    notes = d.get('notes', [])
    if not notes:
        continue
    first = notes[0]
    resolved = d.get('resolved', None)
    resolvable = d.get('resolvable', False)
    if resolvable and resolved:
        continue  # skip resolved
    print(f'=== Discussion {d[\"id\"]} | resolvable={resolvable} | resolved={resolved} ===')
    for n in notes:
        pos = n.get('position', {})
        if pos:
            print(f'  File: {pos.get(\"new_path\", pos.get(\"old_path\",\"\"))} line {pos.get(\"new_line\", pos.get(\"old_line\",\"\"))}')
        print(f'  Author: {n[\"author\"][\"username\"]} @ {n[\"created_at\"]}')
        print(f'  Body: {n[\"body\"]}')
        print()
"
input.description: Fetch MR discussions and filter to unresolved ones

lastPrompt: get the unresolved comments on the MR

content.text:
Here are the 6 unresolved inline comments:

---

**1. `audit_logging.py` line 201** — LyndonHB
> I think this retry stuff would be best isolated a little more into its own code.
> I am also unsure at a glance what the `time.sleep` call does. Why are we doing a left bitwise shift?

The bitwise left shift (`1 << attempt`) is exponential backoff — `1, 2, 4, 8, 16` seconds. Reviewer wants this extracted and the intent clarified.

---

**2. `request_processor.py` line 189** — LyndonHB
> What's the point in this empty case?

The `case "done": pass` I added to make the match non-dead-ended — reviewer is questioning it.

---

**3. `request_processor.py` line 201** — LyndonHB
> Should this not be called by the `case "done"` branch?

Reviewer is suggesting the archiver call should live inside the match's `"done"` case rather than after the match block.

---

**4. `audit_logging.py` line 312** — LyndonHB
> Perhaps sort by timestamp so this is consistent.

The entries returned from `get_audit_log` should be sorted by timestamp.

---

**5. `creator_lambda.py` line 157** — LyndonHB
> We should take a note and make `request_repo` throw a more sensible exception so it is easier to know what has happened (i.e. a duplicate key was raised)

The raw `ConditionalCheckFailedException` catch in the lambda should eventually be moved into the repository layer as a domain exception. This is a note/TODO comment on the current approach.

---

**6. `README.md` line 111** — LyndonHB
> Might want to move this around in the README.

Section placement feedback on the README content I added.

lastPrompt: get the unresolved comments on the MR
